Name: BIOS Attribute Registry
Description: This registry defines a representation of BIOS Attribute classes, menus, and properties for RackServer RS3600 G3 (BMC firmware v4.2.1)

Attributes.HelpText: Current BIOS firmware version installed on the system. Read-only value updated after firmware upgrade.
Current BMC firmware version. Provides out-of-band management and monitoring capabilities.
Unique system serial number assigned at manufacturing for warranty and asset tracking.
User-defined asset tag string for inventory management. Can be set via Redfish or BIOS setup.
Total physical memory detected during POST. Read from DIMM SPD data across all populated channels.
Enables Intel Hyper-Threading Technology. Each physical core appears as two logical processors to the OS.
Attributes.WarningText: Changing this setting requires a system reset to take effect.

Attributes.HelpText: Intel Virtualization Technology for IA-32. Provides hardware-assisted CPU virtualization required by hypervisors.
Intel VT for Directed I/O. Enables DMA remapping for I/O device virtualization. Required for PCIe passthrough.
Intel Turbo Boost Technology dynamically increases processor frequency when thermal and power headroom permits.
Enables processor C-States for power saving when cores are idle.
Number of active processor cores per socket. Reducing core count lowers power consumption.
Intel AES-NI instruction set for hardware-accelerated AES encryption/decryption.
Intel Advanced Vector Extensions for high-throughput floating-point and integer computations.
MLC stream prefetcher. Detects sequential streams and prefetches into L2 cache.
MLC spatial prefetcher. Fetches adjacent cache lines for spatial locality workloads.
Data Cache Unit stream prefetcher. Detects sequential load patterns for L1 data cache.
DCU instruction-pointer prefetcher. Uses load history to predict future addresses.
Last Level Cache (L3) prefetcher. Prefetches into shared LLC to reduce off-chip latency.
Ultra Path Interconnect link data rate. Auto negotiates optimal rate.
UPI reference clock frequency select. Higher frequencies increase inter-socket bandwidth.
Memory operating frequency in Mega Transfers per second. Auto mode trains optimal speed from DIMM SPD.
Enables NUMA-aware memory topology reporting to the OS via ACPI SRAT.
Error Correction Code for system memory. Provides single-bit error correction and multi-bit error detection.
POST memory test mode. Full performs comprehensive testing. Fast performs quick test. Disabled skips test.
Background memory patrol scrub. Periodically reads and corrects single-bit errors.
Patrol scrub interval in hours. Shorter intervals increase correction frequency.
Corrects single-bit errors on demand reads. Detected errors are corrected immediately.
Asynchronous DRAM Refresh with data save. On power loss, saves critical memory content.
Memory interleave block size across channels.
Number of memory channels to interleave for parallel bandwidth.
Full memory mirroring duplicates writes to two channels. Survives complete channel failure.
Reserves a spare DRAM rank per channel to replace a failing rank.
SATA controller operation mode. AHCI provides NCQ. RAID enables firmware RAID. Disabled turns off SATA.
NVMe SSD management mode. BIOS mode for legacy, OS Native for direct OS control.
Enables S.M.A.R.T. monitoring and reporting during POST. Warns about impending drive failures.
Preboot eXecution Environment support. Enables network boot via DHCP/TFTP for OS deployment.
UEFI HTTP/HTTPS boot support for cloud and datacenter provisioning.
Enables remote system wake-up via Magic Packet over Ethernet.
System power and performance policy. Performance maximizes throughput, Balanced optimizes ratio, Power Saving prioritizes efficiency.
Enables dynamic power capping to limit system power consumption.
Maximum system power consumption in watts when power capping is enabled.
Intel Enhanced SpeedStep. Enables dynamic processor frequency and voltage scaling.
Intel Speed Shift Technology. Hardware-controlled P-state transitions.
Hints the processor to optimize for performance or power efficiency.
Maximum package C-State allowed. Deeper states save more power but increase exit latency.
Maximum PCI Express link speed.
PCI Express Advanced Error Reporting for detailed error logging and recovery.
Access Control Services. Prevents PCIe peer-to-peer transactions. Required for secure device passthrough.
Single Root I/O Virtualization. Exposes virtual functions for direct VM assignment.
Enables PCIe MMIO decoding above 4 GB. Required for GPUs and accelerators with large BARs.
Master control for all USB ports. Disabling turns off all USB controllers for security.
XHCI USB controller hand-off to OS. Transfers USB ownership to OS during POST.
System boot mode. UEFI provides modern firmware with Secure Boot and GPT. Legacy for older OS.
Minimizes POST time by skipping certain hardware initialization.
Displays OEM logo instead of POST diagnostic messages during boot.
Sets the NumLock key state at system boot.
First boot device in the boot order sequence.
Second boot device. Attempted if first device fails.
Third boot device.
BIOS administrator password. Required for full setup access. Not retrievable via Redfish.
BIOS user password. Allows limited boot-time access.
Require password entry on every system boot.
Trusted Platform Module enable. Required for Secure Boot, BitLocker, and measured boot.
TPM specification version. TPM 2.0 required for Windows 11 and modern security.
Clears TPM ownership and stored keys. All TPM-protected data becomes inaccessible.
UEFI Secure Boot. Prevents loading of unsigned UEFI drivers and OS bootloaders.
Secure Boot operational mode. Standard uses factory keys. Custom allows user-defined key management.
Intel Software Guard Extensions. Creates hardware-encrypted enclaves for confidential computing.
Enclave Page Cache memory reservation size for Intel SGX.
BMC network address assignment method. Static requires manual IP, DHCP obtains automatically.
BMC IPv4 address when IP Source is set to Static.
